activities_projects.title: Advanced Data Collection Project
activities_projects.objective: Collect large real-life data set and organize it
steps: Choose a topic (e.g., daily temperatures, footfall in school, household water usage)
Collect data for at least 50 entries
Tabulate the data in classes or intervals
Draw frequency, cumulative frequency tables
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops skills for handling large data sets
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and completeness of data (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparison of Multiple Data Sets
activities_projects.objective: Analyze and compare multiple groups using statistical measures
steps: Take data from three different classes (e.g., marks in a test)
Calculate mean, median, and mode for each class
Draw comparative bar graphs and cumulative frequency curves
Write observations on which class performed better and why
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculation, graphs, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Box-and-Whisker Plot Creation
activities_projects.objective: Represent grouped data using a box plot
steps: Take data for a group of students (e.g., heights or marks)
Calculate quartiles Q1, Q2 (median), Q3
Draw box-and-whisker plot to visualize spread and central tendency
Analyze data skewness and range
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes data distribution using advanced plots
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct quartiles, plot, and interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Probability Connection Project
steps: Collect data from tossing dice or coins multiple times
Record frequency of each outcome
Calculate experimental probability
Compare with theoretical probability and analyze differences
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct probability calculations and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Statistical Analysis of Real-Life Survey
activities_projects.objective: Conduct survey and analyze using statistical measures
steps: Conduct survey on topics like favourite food, mode of transport, or study hours
Organize data in frequency tables
Calculate mean, median, mode, and range
Draw bar graphs, pie charts, and cumulative frequency curves
Write observations and conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies all statistical tools to real-life situations
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness, correct calculations, and graphical representation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Time-Series Data Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Collect and analyze data over time
steps: Take data like daily rainfall, stock prices, or temperatures over 30 days
Draw time-series graph with days on x-axis and values on y-axis
Calculate trends, averages, and fluctuations
Interpret results
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate graph, calculations, and interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Grouped Data Analysis with Cumulative Frequencies
activities_projects.objective: Calculate median, quartiles, and mode for grouped data
steps: Take data in class intervals
Calculate cumulative frequencies
Determine median using median formula
Calculate quartiles Q1 and Q3
Calculate mode using modal class formula
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Advanced calculation skills for grouped data
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct results for median, quartiles, and mode (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Correlation Analysis Project
steps: Collect paired data (e.g., hours studied vs marks obtained)
Draw scatter diagram
Observe trend and discuss correlation (positive/negative/no correlation)
Write findings
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct scatter plot and interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Advanced Pie Chart Project
activities_projects.objective: Represent complex categorical data using pie chart
steps: Collect categorical data (e.g., students’ favourite subjects or sports)
Calculate angles for each category
Draw pie chart accurately using protractor
Label each sector and write percentage values
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct angles, labeling, and percentages (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comprehensive Statistical Project
activities_projects.objective: Combine data collection, tabulation, graphing, and analysis
steps: Select a topic for survey (e.g., daily habits of students)
Collect data for at least 50 entries
Create frequency and cumulative frequency tables
Calculate mean, median, mode, and range
Draw bar graphs, pie charts, and cumulative frequency curves
Interpret data and write detailed observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates all statistical methods in one comprehensive project
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, completeness, graphical representation, and analysis (10 marks)
